Easy bruising resident survival guide

# Overview
Easy bruising is the bruising of skin with minor compression or pressure. When small capillaries of the superficial skin rupture from pressure or compression, petechiae occur, sometimes ecchymosis is also seen with insignifican...

## Moderate to Severe Bruises
If bruising is severe, (harm score 2-3) it may be dangerous, or it can cause serious complications.  Further bleeding and excess fluid may accumulate causing a hard, fluctuating lump or swelling hematoma.  This has the potential to cause compartment syndrome as the swel...

## Light Bruises

case_memory.prior_information_summary: 69-year-old male, admitted via clinic referral, no prior reported issues. Objective: Assess and diagnose based on clinical presentation. Final result: Acquired coagulation factor deficiency. Knowledge base includes Von Willebrand disease, Activated protein C resistance, and Antiphospholipid syndrome. No lab, imaging, or microbiology results found. Multiple tool outputs returned no results for labs, CXR, imaging, microbiology.
